provenance.source_corpus: Gauging recovery of zooplankton from historical acid and metal contamination: the influence of temporal changes in restoration targets

source_spans.text: As water quality improved, zooplankton communities changed in the Sudbury lakes.
As water quality improved, zooplankton communities changed in the Sudbury lakes.

Changes in climate, acid deposition and lakeshore development have altered the thermal regime and water quality of the reference lakes over the last 25 years (Palmer et al. 2011; M.E. Palmer, N.D. Yan & K.M. Somers, unpublished data), and correlated shifts in their zooplankton communities have occurred (Yan et al. 2008).

Increases in richness were significant (Mann – Kendall trend tests Z-values >4/C16, P-values <0/C1001) and were correlated with increases in pH and decreases in copper and nickel (Spearman rank order correlation coefﬁcients = 0/C160– 0/C192, P-values <0/C1001).

Increases in richness were significant (Mann – Kendall trend tests Z-values >4/C16, P-values <0/C1001) and were correlated with increases in pH and decreases in copper and nickel (Spearman rank order correlation coefﬁcients = 0/C160– 0/C192, P-values <0/C1001).

Increases in richness were significant (Mann – Kendall trend tests Z-values >4/C16, P-values <0/C1001) and were correlated with increases in pH and decreases in copper and nickel (Spearman rank order correlation coefﬁcients = 0/C160– 0/C192, P-values <0/C1001).

Increases in richness were significant (Mann – Kendall trend tests Z-values >4/C16, P-values <0/C1001) and were correlated with increases in pH and decreases in copper and nickel (Spearman rank order correlation coefﬁcients = 0/C160– 0/C192, P-values <0/C1001).

Changes in climate, acid deposition and lakeshore development have altered the thermal regime and water quality of the reference lakes over the last 25 years (Palmer et al. 2011; M.E. Palmer, N.D. Yan & K.M. Somers, unpublished data), and correlated shifts in their zooplankton communities have occurred (Yan et al. 2008).

Changes in climate, acid deposition and lakeshore development have altered the thermal regime and water quality of the reference lakes over the last 25 years (Palmer et al. 2011; M.E. Palmer, N.D. Yan & K.M. Somers, unpublished data), and correlated shifts in their zooplankton communities have occurred (Yan et al. 2008).

Changes in climate, acid deposition and lakeshore development have altered the thermal regime and water quality of the reference lakes over the last 25 years (Palmer et al. 2011; M.E. Palmer, N.D. Yan & K.M. Somers, unpublished data), and correlated shifts in their zooplankton communities have occurred (Yan et al. 2008).
Zooplankton recovery was also likely to be limited by continued metal contamination.
small, planktivorous Yellow Perch Perca flavescens have become very abundant in Sudbury lakes, perhaps contributing to poor daphniid recovery (Yan et al. 2004; Valois, Keller & Ramcharan 2010).
small, planktivorous Yellow Perch Perca flavescens have become very abundant in Sudbury lakes, perhaps contributing to poor daphniid recovery (Yan et al. 2004; Valois, Keller & Ramcharan 2010).
